LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DEALYZE INC., A FLORIDA CORPORATION'S MOTION TO VACATE THE ENFORCEMENT ORDER OF OCTOBER 30, 2020 IS GRANTED. THE ORDER IS VOID AND DEFENDANT IS ENTITLED TO RELIEF UNDER CCP 473(D). PRIOR TO THE DISMISSAL OF AUGUST 28, 2018, THE PARTIES DID NOT SUBMIT A REQUEST TO THE COURT TO RETAIN JURISDICTION TO ENFORCE THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT PER CCP 664.6. THEREFORE, THE VOLUNTARY DISMISSAL DIVESTED THE COURT OF JURISDICTION AND IT COULD NOT RULE ON THE ENFORCEMENT MOTION. (SEE WACKEEN V. MALIS (2002) 97 CAL.APP. 4TH 429, 433.) COUNSEL FOR THE DEFENDANT IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, JR.; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: CHRISTINE L. JORDAN, CSR NO. 12262, CJORDAN@SFTC.ORG (302/RBU)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO VACATE DISMISSAL UNDER CCP 473 AND TO ENTER JUDGMENT UNDER CCP 664.6 IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO SHOW THAT DEFENDANT DEALYZE INC., A FLORIDA CORPORATION, CANNOT OPPOSE THIS MOTION. SEE MINUTES FOR FULL RULING. COUNSEL FOR THE DEFENDANT IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, JR.; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: CHRISTINE L. JORDAN, CSR NO. 12262, CJORDAN@SFTC.ORG (302/RBU)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion To Vacate Dismissal Under Ccp 473 And To Enter Judgment Under Ccp 664.6
Off Calendar Jan-08-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. THOMAS J. LALANNE, Esq. of Law Offices of Thomas J. LaLanne (Tel: 415-434-1122) for plaintiffs Mark Evans and Leslie Evans;
Atty. KORAY J. BULUT, Esq. of Goodwin Procter LLP (Tel: 415-733-6000) for defendants Dealyze, Inc., a Florida Corporation, Dealyze Inc., a California Corporation, and Dealyze, Inc., a Delaware Corporation.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF MARK EVANS AND LESLIE EVANS' Motion To Vacate Dismissal Under CCP 473 And To Enter Judgment Under CCP 664.6.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling but removes the sentence "First, those arguments are untimely as they are raised for the first time in the reply," as follows:

Plaintiffs' motion to vacate is denied. Plaintiff fails to show that defendant Dealyze Inc., a Florida Corporation, cannot oppose this motion.

Plaintiffs are not entitled to discretionary relief under CCP 473(b) because the dismissal was entered on August 28, 2018 and plaintiffs filed their motion for relief on November 30, 2020, which is more than six months after entry of the dismissal. (See Arambula v. Union Carbide Corp. (2005) 128 Cal.App.4th 333, 345 [six-month period mandatory and court has no authority to grant relief from that limitation].) Plaintiffs do not expressly seek relief under the mandatory portion of CCP 473(b), but the LaLanne declaration could be construed as an attorney affidavit of fault. The timing provision for mandatory relief is different from the discretionary relief portion of the statute because it runs from "entry of judgment." Plaintiffs cannot obtain mandatory relief because the dismissal with prejudice constitutes the judgment. (See Roybal v. University Ford (1989) 207 Cal.App.3d 1080, 1087 ["dismissal with prejudice of the municipal court action constituted a final judgment"].) The court rejects plaintiffs' arguments that they are entitled to relief based on equity (extrinsic fraud or mistake) or clerical error. Neither of those bases for relief applies to the circumstances of this case.

Plaintiff must file a separate action to enforce the settlement. (See Wackeen v. Malis (2002) 97 Cal.App.4th 429, 433, ["[i]f, after a suit has been dismissed, a party brings a section 664.6 motion for a judgment on a settlement agreement but cannot present to the court a request for retention of jurisdiction that meets all of these requirements, then enforcement of the agreement must be left to a separate lawsuit."].)

Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Christine L. Jordan, CSR No. 12262, cjordan@sftc.org (302/RBU)

Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Or, In The Alternative, Reconsider Order Granting Enforcement Of Settlement Under Ccp 664.6
Off Calendar Jan-08-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. THOMAS J. LALANNE, Esq. of Law Offices of Thomas J. LaLanne (Tel: 415-434-1122) for plaintiffs Mark Evans and Leslie Evans;
Atty. KORAY J. BULUT, Esq. of Goodwin Procter LLP (Tel: 415-733-6000) for defendants Dealyze, Inc., a Florida Corporation, Dealyze Inc., a California Corporation, and Dealyze, Inc., a Delaware Corporation.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS DEALYZE INC., A FLORIDA CORPORATION, AND DEALYZE INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION'S Motion To Vacate Or, In The Alternative, Reconsider Order Granting Enforcement Of Settlement Under Ccp 664.6.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Dealyze Inc., a Florida Corporation's motion to vacate the enforcement order of October 30, 2020 is granted. The order is void and defendant is entitled to relief under CCP 473(d). Prior to the dismissal of August 28, 2018, the parties did not submit a request to the Court to retain jurisdiction to enforce the settlement agreement per CCP 664.6. Therefore, the voluntary dismissal divested the Court of jurisdiction and it could not rule on the enforcement motion. (See Wackeen v. Malis (2002) 97 Cal.App. 4th 429, 433.)

Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Christine L. Jordan, CSR No. 12262, cjordan@sftc.org (302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-15-2018 Set For Trial On Mar-18-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-17-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT DEALYZE INC., A FLORIDA CORPORATION, DEALYZE INC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFF'S 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendants' motion to strike the second amended complaint and request for sanctions is denied. The motion should not have been filed and is an unfortunate example of why civil litigation has become so expensive and fractious. Any concern or uncertainty by defendants' counsel whether plaintiffs intended to proceed with a second amended complaint - that had been timely served but, through excusable mistake, not timely filed - could and should have been resolved by a simple inquiry to plaintiffs' counsel. The aspersions cast on plaintiffs and their counsel in the moving and reply papers are unwarranted. Plaintiffs' counsel has shown admirable restraint by not seeking sanctions.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. =(302/HK)
